name: Xero Accounting API
what_it_does: Xero is a cloud accounting platform for small and medium businesses. Its API provides programmatic access to invoices, contacts, bank transactions, expenses, payroll, fixed assets, and financial reports, enabling accounting automation and integrations.
use_cases: Automated invoice creation and payment tracking
Expense claim processing and approval workflows
not_for: Large enterprise ERP needs (Xero targets SMBs; lacks some enterprise features)
US payroll (Xero Payroll is limited to certain regions)
Real-time payment processing (use Stripe or similar for payments)
best_when: An agent needs to manage accounting data, generate invoices, or reconcile transactions for a small or medium business already using Xero.
avoid_when: The business uses QuickBooks, Sage, or other accounting software — data migration adds significant friction.

auth.notes: OAuth2 with PKCE for user authorization. Scopes are granular per resource area (accounting.transactions, accounting.contacts, payroll.employees, etc.). Tokens expire after 30 minutes; refresh tokens valid for 60 days. Multi-tenant support — one app can connect to multiple Xero organisations, each with its own tenantId that must be passed on every API call.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Demo company available in sandbox for free development testing
pricing.paid_tiers: Starter $29/mo (limited invoices/bills); Standard $46/mo (unlimited); Premium $62/mo (multi-currency). API access included on all plans.
pricing.notes: 30-day free trial available. App connections require the merchant to have an active Xero subscription. Partner apps can be built for free but require merchant subscription for production use.

performance.rate_limits: 60 API calls/minute per app per Xero organisation. Daily limit of 5000 calls per organisation. Minute limit resets every 60 seconds.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Xero returns ValidationErrors array for 400 errors with Message and Detail fields. HTTP 429 for rate limits includes Retry-After header. Error codes are not always machine-readable codes — often human-readable strings. Some validation errors are very detailed; others are vague.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency key mechanism. Agents creating invoices or transactions must check for existing records before creating to avoid duplicates. The Contact and Invoice models have unique reference fields that can be queried first.
known_agent_gotchas: tenantId must be passed on every API call — missing this is the most common agent error
Access tokens expire after 30 minutes — agents must implement token refresh proactively
60 calls/minute per organisation limit is easy to hit when syncing large datasets
Modified-since filtering is critical for incremental sync — otherwise agents re-fetch all data
Xero uses GUID-based IDs everywhere — agents must resolve names to GUIDs before referencing
Multi-tenant apps must store and manage tenantId separately from the OAuth token
Some fields are region-specific (TaxType values vary by country) — hardcoding values causes cross-region failures
Webhook verification requires HMAC-SHA256 signature checking; event delivery is not guaranteed exactly-once

score_rationale: Xero's API is well-documented with a good SDK ecosystem, but several friction points hurt agent usability: mandatory tenantId on every call, 30-minute access token expiry requiring proactive refresh, no idempotency keys, and page-based pagination. The 60 calls/minute rate limit is restrictive for bulk operations. The multi-tenant architecture is powerful for accounting integrators but adds state management burden for agents. Strong choice when the business already uses Xero; less compelling otherwise.
